page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, supported by an L3 cache size of 260 MB, L2 cache of 2 MB, and L1d cache of 48 KB. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-threaded operations. The instance achieves top 10% tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.04 GB/sec, single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.86 GB/sec, and Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2317.0. Text processing also ranks in the top tier at 3051.0. Multi-core and memory benchmarks generally fall within the average middle 50% range, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 8819.0, a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 8777.46, and a static web serving throughput of 17.08 GB/sec. Large block uncached memory read bandwidth is measured at 70.65 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the c4-highcpu-8 is its low memory-to-CPU ratio and the complete absence of local storage, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or massive local data caching. However, the dedicated Intel Xeon processor with Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) provides strong resource density for compute-bound tasks. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, single-threaded data compression, and web serving, as well as lightweight machine learning inference where a GPU is not required.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. It features a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, a 260 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-threaded performance, notably in gzip compression at 35.04 GB/sec and Geekbench single-core execution. Multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and web serving throughput align with average tier expectations. The lack of local storage and low memory allocation represent key tradeoffs, positioning this instance as a resource-dense option for compute-bound tasks rather than memory-heavy applications. It is best suited for software compilation, text processing, single-threaded compression, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-core compression and text processing performance.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier single-core performance and advanced vector extensions for processing-heavy workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz.
16 GB of RAM providing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier single-threaded performance in compression and text processing.
No local storage or integrated GPU hardware included.
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and compute-bound tasks.
